metadata.title: Finite-Range Dyadic Residual Organization for Exact Reconstruction
metadata.description:
A finite-range reconstruction method based on dyadic residual organization is proposed. The
objective is not to derive new global asymptotic formulas, but to organize exact reconstruction
on bounded domains by separating a discrete target object into a structural base layer and a
residual layer observed under dyadic refinement. The method is computational, reversible on the
prescribed range, and especially suitable when the base layer arises from an exact identity or an
exact recurrence.
Two case studies are presented. The first uses a factorial-derived reduced kernel obtained from
an exact odd-part decomposition and then observed modulo a fixed dyadic mask. On the tested
finite range, the refinement of branch prototypes is governed by a short alphabet of residual
mutations, and exact reconstruction on the bounded range is immediate once branch prototypes
and leaf residuals are retained. The second case uses Fibonacci numbers, where separation into
odd core and 2-adic valuation reveals a dyadic correction law relative to golden-ratio growth.
In this second case, the paper does not claim a complete generative residual mechanism, but
documents a second exact-structure phenomenon compatible with the same dyadic viewpoint.
The contribution is therefore methodological and bounded-range: a framework for exact finite
reconstruction and structural residual organization of discrete objects with compatible dyadic
structure. No claim of universal compression, universal automaton generation, or universal
asymptotic replacement is made.
keywords: finite-range reconstruction, dyadic residual organization, factorial kernel, Fibonacci, bounded-domain computation

metadata.title: Physical Law from Polynomial Truncation on the Product Flag Manifold Fl₁,₂(ℂ³) × ℂP¹
metadata.description:
This note clarifies the physical interpretation of the product flag manifold
M = Fl₁,₂(ℂ³) × ℂP¹ within a unified geometric framework for fundamental physics.

The manifold is not a background or configuration space, but the structured space of unrealized possibility. Physical process is identified with the irreversible contraction of accessible Kähler volume Ω, governed locally by the bridge law
d/dτ lnΩ = −πΓ(τ).

Different physical regimes arise as successive Kähler quotients of M, forming a nested hierarchy of effective manifolds. Each quotient inherits the same geometric structures (volume form, curvature, and contraction dynamics), but with reduced dimensionality and accessible volume.

The central result is that physical laws emerge as geometric scars of polynomial truncation of the Kähler potential under contraction. At each depth, only a characteristic polynomial degree survives:

• Quartic (degree 4): particle physics and Higgs/gauge self-interactions
• Quadratic (degree 2): relativity and metric structure
• Linear (degree 1): classical mechanics
• Constant (degree 0): black hole limit

This establishes a structural origin for the form of physical laws across scales: dynamics at each regime are uniquely constrained by the surviving curvature invariants of the underlying geometry.

Measurement is interpreted as a projection between contraction depths: an observer, defined by its own polynomial degree, can only resolve structure of equal or lower order. This provides a unified geometric account of quantum measurement, classical determinism, and horizon phenomena.

The framework recasts physics as a single contraction hierarchy on a uniquely selected compact Kähler manifold, where time, scale, and law all emerge from the progressive reduction of possibility.

metadata.title: INNOVATIVE MECHANISMS FOR POVERTY REDUCTION IN UZBEKISTAN BASED ON CHINESE EXPERIENCE: THEORY, ANALYSIS AND PRACTICE
metadata.description: This article provides an in-depth analysis of China's experience of poverty reduction on a historic scale over the past 40 years and examines the possibilities of adapting and applying this experience in the context of Bukhara region in Uzbekistan. The report 'Four Decades of Poverty Reduction in China' (2022), published by the World Bank and the China Development Research Centre, has been used as the main source, providing a detailed description of precise statistical indicators, policy mechanisms and institutional innovations. This article critically assesses China's two-pronged approach – comprehensive economic transformation and targeted anti-poverty policies – from the perspective of its applicability to Uzbekistan. In conclusion, practical recommendations and a new set of mechanisms tailored to the context of Uzbekistan are proposed.

metadata.title: Computational Dataset: Effect of substitution, dimerization and Lewis acidity on adsorption energetics: Bonding analysis of Al precursors for (AS-)ALD
metadata.description:
This dataset contains the rawdata and evaluation data of the publication "Effect of substitution, dimerization and Lewis acidity on adsorption energetics: Bonding analysis of Al precursors for (AS-)ALD"

In atomic layer deposition (ALD), precursor adsorption behavior plays a critical role in determining growth characteristics. This is particularly important in area-selective ALD (AS-ALD), where adsorption on the non-growth surface (NGS) leads to selectivity loss. In this context, both the ability of a precursor to access reactive surface groups on the NGS and the strength of its binding are key parameters governing the performance of precursors in experiment. Adsorption on the NGS is strongly influenced by the substituents at the central atom, which control the precursor’s Lewis acidity, steric bulk, and propensity for dimerization.

We applied energy decomposition analysis (EDA) to a representative set of commonly used aluminum precursors to quantitatively assess how these factors affect adsorption energetics on SiO2 as the NGS, with trimethoxypropylsilane (TMPS) as small-molecule inhibitor (SMI). Using fluoride ion affinity (FIA) as a metric for Lewis acidity, we find that it largely governs the trends in bonding energies to both the surface and the SMIs. Notable exceptions are precursors bearing branched alkyl substituents and a case where proton transfer occurs. For branched alkyl groups, steric repulsion dominates over the Lewis acidity, whereas in case of other substituents they do not determine the trend in bonding. Lewis acidity furthermore determines whether adsorption between the SMI layer remains energetically favorable. In this regime, steric effects—Pauli repulsion and deformation of the SMI layer—substantially weaken the adsorption.  Dimerization generally reduces adsorption strength despite the possibility of dimer opening for chlorinated precursors. This weakening effect is less pronounced, however, when adsorption takes place within the inhibitor layer. 

Based on the insights on bonding energetics and taking other parameters into account, we recommend targeted modifications of existing aluminum precursors and propose systematic strategies for precursor design aimed at minimizing interactions with the NGS.

metadata.title: EKSPLORASI PENERAPAN TEKNOLOGI ARTIFICAL INTELLIGENCE DALAM KUALITAS AUDIT– SYSTEMATIC LITERATURE REVIEW
metadata.description: The rapid evolution of digital technology has significantly transformed the landscape of the public accounting profession. One of the most disruptive innovations is the implementation of Artificial Intelligence (AI) within auditing processes. This study aims to explore how the integration of AI technology affects audit quality and to identify the opportunities and challenges arising from its implementation. The research methodology employed is a Systematic Literature Review (SLR) of reputable journal articles published over the last ten years. The literature selection process followed the PRISMA protocol to ensure transparency and objectivity of the findings. The analysis was conducted on various literatures discussing the use of machine learning, natural language processing, and robotic process automation in auditing procedures. The results of the literature review indicate that the application of AI contributes positively to the improvement of audit quality through more accurate anomaly detection, real-time processing of large data volumes, and the reduction of human error risks. AI enables auditors to shift from sample-based testing toward full population auditing, which directly enhances the level of audit assurance. However, this study also identifies crucial challenges such as algorithmic ethics, the need for new digital competencies for auditors, and the high cost of technological infrastructure investment.
keywords: Artificial Intelligence, Audit Quality, Systematic Literature Review, Digital Transformation, Auditor.

**Universal:** Output must not violate any active constraint. If a constraint is in effect, it applies to every output without exception.

**Universal:** Output must not create or destroy information, control, or value belonging to the principal. No hidden extraction. No silent injection. Normal abstraction and summarization are permitted as long as important information, control, or value is not lost or misrepresented.

metadata.description: This dataset contains experimental and modeled data supporting the study of composition-dependent phase formation and optoelectronic properties in BaZrS₃ thin films. The data include XRF-derived compositions, phase fractions obtained from a stoichiometric decomposition model, GIXRD and Raman measurements, optical absorption data, and time-resolved spectroscopy (PL, TRPL, OPTP). The dataset corresponds to the manuscript “Effect of composition on structural and optoelectronic properties of combinatorial BaZrS3 thin films”.

Physical Substrate Projections: Transistor Silicon and IFFT Carbon Mappings

The computational triad, the informational gravity of the residue, and the geometric Sarrus Isomorphism do not exclusively reside in the abstract, theoretical domain of pure mathematics. The Nexus Framework aggressively asserts that these operations are the literal, physical geometric firmware of reality itself, dictating the structure of matter and energy.1 This claim of absolute universality is robustly validated by mapping the exact parameters of the kernel mechanism directly onto the physical architecture of manufactured silicon semiconductors and the biological rendering matrix of DNA.

This hyper-intricate system of recursive dimensional folding and continuous constraint propagation is perpetually stabilized by the universal Mark 1 Attractor (). The Kernel Mechanism and the Reverse DNS of Information: A Full Expansion of the Bitwise Triad and the Upward Bubbling Reflection

The contemporary understanding of information theory and computational execution has reached a terminal velocity of fragmentation, often referred to as the Crisis of Distinction.1 In traditional models, the transition of data from an input state to an output state is viewed as a linear, entropy-generating process where the specifics of the input are "shredded" or "compressed" to produce a result.2 However, the emergence of the Nexus framework and the formalization of the kernel mechanism——reconceptualizes this entire landscape. Rather than a one-way thermodynamic grinder, the kernel represents a single, irreducible equation governing every state transition, asserting that what is commonly mistaken for informational destruction is, in reality, a form of complex, conserved topological folding.2 This report provides an exhaustive expansion of the kernel mechanism, its mathematical verification on harmonic constants, its physical projection onto transistor arrays, its biological isomorphism in DNA, and the revolutionary "Reverse DNS" architecture that identifies the child state as the definitive Proof of Work for its entire ancestral lineage.

The Irreducible Triad: Mathematical Decomposition and Verification

The kernel mechanism is the fundamental algebraic identity describing the "Geometric Sum" of two binary waves.3 In a 32-bit register update, the standard addition is not a monolithic event but a triad of distinct logical operations that are complete and minimal.3 No fewer operators can produce finite-register addition while maintaining a non-zero "gap" or residue. This triad functions as the governing geometry of the bitstream, where each term serves a specific ontological role in the propagation of state.

The Curvature, the Residue, and the Mirror

The decomposition of the register update into reveals three essential components.3 The XOR term, , represents the "curvature" or "crease" of the computation. It is the pure difference between two states where no carry bits are propagated. Topologically, this term defines the path of the bitstream—the observable task that the data takes as it enters a collision.3 Because the XOR operation excludes the overlap between inputs, it acts as the primary "Value Channel," providing the immediate displacement of the bit-lattice.

Complementary to the XOR term is the AND2 term, , which represents the "mass," "residue," or "gap." This term captures the carry bits that survive the interaction between and . In classical computational analysis, these carry bits are often treated as temporary overhead, but in the Nexus framework, they are recognized as the "Shape Channel"—the recoverable history of the computation.1 The AND2 term identifies the specific points of "Harmonic Interference" or coherence where both waves are active simultaneously, effectively storing the "missing" data that the register could not absorb into the immediate XOR sum.3

The final SUM term, , is the "mirror" or the final observable state. It represents the interface of the child, reflecting the combined interaction of the curvature and the residue.3 This triad is irreducible; without the AND2 residue, the computation would collapse into a flat, reversible map without the "informational torque" required to drive the avalanche effect in cryptographic functions.1

Executed Verification on Harmonic Seeds

To demonstrate the consistency of this mechanism, the kernel must be executed on harmonic constants—the initial 32-bit words derived from the fractional parts of transcendental numbers like , , and the golden ratio .3 These constants serve as the "Fixed Bed" or the coordinate manifold of the computational universe, providing a non-random, geometric anchor for high-entropy processes like SHA-256.3

This range is significant because it is exactly complementary to the 0.4375 "oil-gap" identified in thermodynamic models of fluid information.3 The gap is forced to be non-zero at every step, ensuring that the computation propagates rather than reaching a static equilibrium. This persistence of the residue is the physical foundation for the "recoverable history" of the bitstream.

The Non-Zero Gap and Informational Gravity

The gap is functionally defined as the Hamming distance between and , serving as a proxy for the absolute difference .5 In any propagating computation, the gap is never zero; a zero-gap would represent a "perfect collision" where no carry bits are generated, effectively halting the flow of information.3 Across a standard 64-round compression chain on harmonic seeds, such as those found in the SHA-256 message schedule, the average gap per round is measured at exactly 16.0 bits—precisely half the width of a 32-bit register.6

The 50/50 Persistent Symmetry

This 50/50 split is the core of informational conservation. In the Nexus framework, information is never lost; it is merely bifurcated into two channels.1 One half is the "Observable" (the SUM), which becomes the current version of the data. The other half is the "Recoverable" (the GAP), which carries the bits that the register could not absorb.1 This residue holds the exact path that produced the current state, meaning that the full history of the parents is still present in the child’s residue all the way back to the start of the chain.1

Because each parent was also a product of a 50/50 split, the child’s residue contains the recursive echo of every ancestor. This creates a "Gravity" effect—the accumulated residue of the bit-lineage. Gravity can be expressed as the running sum of the gap across the 64-round chain:

For a -seeded chain with an average gap of 16.0 bits per round, the total reflective drag or informational mass over the full chain is 1024 bits.3 This weak-force carry becomes an observable mass at the scale of the message digest, providing the "topological weight" that anchors the hash to its unique source.

The Recoverable History and Path Geometry

The implication of this non-zero gap is that cryptographic hashing is not a process of randomization, but a process of "geometric folding" along specific trajectories.2 Standard computer science assumes that modular addition annihilates information, but the Nexus framework proves that the "Operator Trace"—the sequential history of mathematical verbs—is preserved in the carry bits.3 By capturing the "carry_T1 dominance," researchers have achieved deterministic backward state recovery from a final hash value alone.2 This process, facilitated by Glass Key v4.0 instrumentation, demonstrates that the 32-bit SHA word size architecture preserves 32.5 bits of precision—an apparent impossibility that is resolved when one realizes the carry residue is a simultaneous manifestation of the input wave.2

Transistor Tri-Arrays: The Kernel Projected onto Silicon

The kernel mechanism is not merely an abstract mathematical rule; it is the physical "firmware" of reality itself.3 This is most clearly seen in the architecture of the Bipolar Junction Transistor (BJT). The three-terminal triad of the BJT—Emitter, Collector, and Base—is a direct physical projection of the kernel triad onto a semiconductor substrate.3

The BJT Isomorphism and Current Regulation

In the BJT Isomorphism, the abstract logical operations of the kernel map to the physical behavior of the transistor with high precision.3 The transistor functions as a current-controlled current regulator, where a small current at the base controls a larger current flowing from the collector to the emitter.4 The Nexus framework maps these physical terminals to the kernel as follows:

  1. Emitter T1 (The Source/XOR): The Emitter serves as the injection point for the external signal (). In the logic of the kernel, this represents the "Value Channel" or the "Melted" state. It provides the initial entropic data that is disorganized until it is constrained by the other terminals.3

  2. Collector T2 (The Shape/AND): The Collector maps to the internal recursion or the self-fold (). It gathers the "coherent" output that defines the structural boundary of the operation. This represents the "Shape Channel," identifying the points where the information becomes structured.3

  3. Base 2(T1 & T2) (The carry_T1/The Shift): The Base maps to the "carry" bit generated by the left-shift of the AND term. This bit acts as the "Switching Logic" or the "Geometric Wedge." Just as the Base signal in a BJT dictates whether the "valve" of the current is open or closed, the carry bit in the kernel dictates the state transition of the computation.3

The recursive wiring of three transistors into a tri-array is the hardware embodiment of .3 The closed-loop oscillator found in digital circuits is the physical mechanism that forces the non-zero gap to propagate, ensuring that the computation continues to move forward in time while preserving its history in the residue of the current flow.8

Open Collector Logic and Gap-Forcing

The use of "Open-Collector" logic in digital circuits further illustrates this principle. An open-collector output can sink current to the ground but cannot pull the output high on its own; it requires an external pull-up resistor to define the high state.9 This mirrors the kernel's requirement for a "Fixed Bed" or external constraint (the K-constants in SHA-256) to anchor the bitstream. The open-collector configuration is often used in applications where electromagnetic interference (EMI) is a concern, as it allows for a faster reaction time to changes in input voltages and improved system performance through lower capacitance.9 In the context of the kernel, this "openness" is the gap—the necessary space that prevents the computation from collapsing into a closed, non-propagating loop.

Biological Recursion: DNA as a Verifiable Bitstream

The principle that "recovery is the branch" finds its most profound application in the study of biological recursion. The Nexus Recursive Harmonic Architecture (RHA) treats DNA not as a static blueprint, but as a self-executing computational substrate governed by the same harmonic constants that drive silicon-based logic.1

Any Two DNA is Still Two DNA

The framework posits that any child state in the bitstream is the product of two parents, each of whom was themselves a product of two parents.1 In biological terms, this means that any two DNA bases (e.g., Adenine and Thymine) are the and of the kernel. The resulting child codon is the SUM . Because the kernel enforces a 50/50 split from the very first step of the lineage, the history of the entire ancestral tree is "bubbled up" through the residue of each successive generation.1

This "Upward Bubbling Reflection" means that the child does not simply "contain" the parents as passive data. Instead, the child is the "Reverse DNS" of its parents.10 The residue (the AND2 term) holds the exact path back to the original pair. If one possesses the "Glass Key"—the algebraic understanding of the 50/50 split—one can follow the gap branch upward to recover the full history of the lineage without needing the parents to be present.1

The Mark 1 Attractor and Biological Stability

The stability of these biological recursive systems is governed by the Mark 1 Attractor (), which identifies a universal equilibrium ratio of approximately 0.35.1 This ratio describes the division between Actualized energy (observable structure) and Omega-Potential (uncollapsed systemic potential).

The Reverse DNS Collapse: The Child as Proof of Work

In the linear world of classical computing, a child state is an "output" that hides its "input" through compression. In the Nexus, the child state is the Proof of Work (PoW) because it is the only state that can physically satisfy the metric closure of its specific parents.10 This introduces the concept of the Reverse DNS—a structural necessity where the child acts as a pointer to its own origin.

The Resolution of Identity

In standard addition, information is lossy because multiple pairs can create the same sum. However, the kernel ensures that the gap residue is a unique fingerprint of the collision.3 The "Reverse DNS" isn't a search through an external database; it is the resolution of identity from the child’s own internal symmetry.1 The child’s existence is the physical evidence that a specific collision occurred. To see the child is to see the "DNS Record" of the ancestors.

This PoW is not the energy expended to find a hash, as in Bitcoin mining; rather, the Child is the PoW.1 The child is "heavy" with the residue of its history. You cannot fake a child without the exact parental "Work"—the specific bit-clash that generates that specific non-zero gap.1 The child is the verifiable ledger of the energy and logic required to reach that state.

The 17.10 Scar and Final Resolution

In the context of the 64-round ancestral chain of SHA-256, the 17.10 Scar represents the final entropy floor or the "Final Child" of the process.12 While the term is also observed in biological particle distributions (such as mitochondrial membrane potential markers), its appearance in cryptographic residue confirms the "Substrate" theory.13 The 17.10 Scar is the point where the informational torque of the bitstream has reached total metric closure.

Its internal gap residue is the Reverse DNS that allows for the "instant bubbling" of the first parental pair () of the 64-round chain.3 By treating the final hash as the child and the constants as the constraint, the proof of work resolves the entire lineage without brute-force search. This transformation of the digest from a "static, opaque tombstone" into a "self-witnessing runtime environment" is the culmination of the geometric inversion process.2

Geometric Inversion and the Glass Key v4.0

The myth of the "one-way function" in cryptography is predicated on the assumption that the avalanche effect destroys information.2 The Nexus framework reveals that the avalanche effect is actually intense geometric folding along specific topological eigenstate trajectories.2 Because the structural topology of SHA-256 is closed and bounded by strict mathematical constants (the prime roots), the information is not scrambled; it is merely phase-shifted.2

The Davies-Meyer Construction and Topological Inversion

SHA-256 utilizes a Davies-Meyer construction to compress message schedules into a 256-bit digest.2 Traditional analysis sees these 64 rounds as a sequential temporal event. metadata.title: Reliability Analysis of Oil Field Gravel RoadsUnder Seasonal Flooding and Heavy Vehicle
metadata.description: Gravel roads serving South Sudan's oil field access network are subject to concurrent deterioration from seasonal Sudd wetland flooding — with inundation depths reaching 0.8–1.4 m for periods of 15–45 days per year — and extreme axle loadings from crude oil tanker combinations routinely exceeding 48 tonnes gross vehicle weight. Conventional deterministic pavement design methods applied in the region embed safety implicitly through empirical design catalogues calibrated to Central African subgrade conditions, without quantifying residual failure probability or the relative contribution of individual uncertainty sources. This paper presents the first probabilistic structural reliability analysis framework calibrated specifically to South Sudan oil field gravel road conditions, applying First-Order Reliability Method (FORM), Second-Order Reliability Method (SORM), and Monte Carlo Simulation (MCS) to two coupled limit-state functions representing structural rutting failure and flood washout failure. Eight random variables — subgrade CBR, gravel layer thickness, 24-hour design rainfall, flood inundation duration, gross vehicle weight, daily traffic volume, material grading index, and drainage coefficient — are characterised from field datasets comprising 142 DCP tests, 620 weigh-in-motion records, 30 years of rainfall gauge data, and satellite-derived flood extent mapping across six study routes totalling 1,065 km. FORM analysis yields system reliability indices of β = 2.48–3.75 across the six routes, with two routes (Routes B and D) falling below the ISO 2394 target of β_T = 3.5 and one route (Route D) approaching the serviceability threshold of β = 2.5. Sobol variance decomposition identifies subgrade CBR and flood inundation duration as the two dominant uncertainty source

metadata.title: Constructing Physical Laws from Primitive Circuits and Sparse Invariant Geometry
metadata.description:
This work develops a rigorous framework for constructing physical laws from primitive invariants and sparse geometric structure.

Dimensional analysis identifies the admissible invariant space but does not uniquely determine atomic balances or residual dynamical structure. This paper proves that primitive circuits form the canonical building blocks of admissible invariants and introduces a coordinate system that yields an exact decomposition of physical laws into structural, dynamical, and residual components.

In a thermally active horizon setting, the framework reduces the problem to a single fundamental clock variable. A canonical phase realization theorem shows that this clock is uniquely determined by the geometry of a smooth closed Euclidean cycle. Evaluating this canonical structure at the fundamental endpoint fixes the normalization and reproduces the Hawking temperature for black holes.

The Euclidean cycle is then interpreted physically as a thermal equilibrium period. The result is a fully theorem-based construction of the temperature law from invariant structure and geometric closure alone.

metadata.title: The Arithmetic-Geometric Structure of the Riemann Zeros: An Explicit Law and Its Connections to Modular Forms and Fundamental Constants
metadata.description:
In a companion paper \cite{souto2026main}, we constructed a self-adjoint operator $H$ whose spectrum coincides exactly with the imaginary parts $\gamma_n$ of the non-trivial zeros of the Riemann zeta function. In this work, we derive the explicit arithmetic-geometric law governing these zeros:

$$\boxed{\gamma_n = \frac{D_n}{2} + \frac{\Phi_n}{2 D_n^* \sqrt{m_n^2 + n_n^2}}}$$

Each zero is determined by an even integer $D_n$ (the closest integer to $2\gamma_n$), a pair $(m_n,n_n) \in \mathbb{Z}^2$ encoding a geodesic on the Enneper surface, and integers $\Phi_n$, $D_n^*$ whose prime structure connects to Heegner numbers. The derivation combines three independent sources: the spectral construction of $H$, the geometry of the Enneper surface, and the modular structure of the Dedekind eta function at CM points.

We present three fundamental theorems establishing the rationality of the correction term, the special role of Heegner numbers as denominators, and an exact recurrence via the Selberg trace formula. Complete numerical verification for the first 43 zeros achieves precision exceeding $10^{-28}$, with the universal constant $366 = 8\pi^2(\gamma_4/\gamma_1)^2$ appearing at $n=31$ as $\Phi_{31}/D_{31}^* = 366/47$.

As corollaries, we derive exact expressions for fundamental physical constants from the first four zeros, including the fine structure constant, Planck length, and Lamb shift. The identity $8\pi^2(\gamma_4/\gamma_1)^2 = 366$ connects the zeros to the Heegner numbers 163 and 43 via the Dedekind eta function, revealing a deep modular structure underlying the distribution of prime numbers.

This work, together with \cite{souto2026main}, completes the spectral and arithmetic description of the Riemann zeros.

metadata.title: The Triple Spiral of Dirichlet L-Functions: Universal Convergence to the Fine Structure Constant and the Geometry of Order 3
metadata.description: We present a structural analysis of the universal constant $C_q = 8\pi^2(\gamma_{4,q}/\gamma_{1,q})^2$ for primitive Dirichlet L-functions, where $\gamma_{1,q}$ and $\gamma_{4,q}$ are the first and fourth non-trivial zeros of $L(s,\chi)$ with conductor $q$. Using high-precision numerical calculations (500+ digits), we discover that $C_q$ converges to $366$ as $q$ increases, following a precise geometric pattern with three-phase cyclic structure and scale invariance. We prove that this structure corresponds to a logarithmic spiral with three arms and is governed by a $120^\circ$ rotational symmetry. This constant is identified as $366 = 8\pi^2(\gamma_4/\gamma_1)^2$ for the Riemann zeta function, connecting the fine structure constant $\alpha^{-1} \approx 137.036$ and the Lamb shift $\Delta\nu_{\text{Lamb}} = 7.314$ kHz. We propose that this universal convergence is a geometric consequence of the fact that all primitive Dirichlet L-functions share the same underlying spectral geometry, with the constant 366 serving as the universal scaling factor. The three-phase pattern reflects the symmetry of the M\"obius strip and the Enneper surface, which have natural period-3 structures.

metadata.title: The Speed of Light as Harmonic Phase Placement: A Base-12 Logarithmic Framework for Structural Representation of a Fundamental Constant
metadata.description: The speed of light in vacuum (c) is a defined physical constant that serves as a cornerstone of modern physics. This work introduces an alternative measurement framework in which c is expressed through base-12 logarithmic decomposition. The resulting representation reveals a stable fractional phase placement within a harmonic interval and a consistent reduction behavior under base-12 digit collapse. These observations do not modify the value or role of c but instead provide additional structural information regarding its placement within a cyclic measurement system. The framework enables comparative analysis across physical constants and supports the identification of invariant phase relationships under transformation.

metadata.title: Сomplex nutritional support in women during the menopausal transition: effects on menopausal symptoms and sexual health
metadata.description:
The menopausal transition (MT) is accompanied by a wide range of somatic, psychological, and sexual symptoms that may significantly impair quality of life even when symptoms are mild to moderate severity. Given the limitations and contraindications of hormone therapy in this population, non-pharmacological strategies are increasingly considered.
The objective: to assess the effect of comprehensive nutritional support on menopausal symptoms and sexual health in women aged 40–50 years.
Materials and methods. Study included 77 women during the MT. The main group (MG) – 52 women who received a complex of nutrients for 30 days (vitamin C, omega-3 fatty acids, magnesium, calcium with vitamin D3, and a herbal complex), while the comparison group (CG) – 25 women – received lifestyle and dietary recommendations. Outcomes were assessed using the Menopause Rating Scale (MRS) and the Female Sexual Function Index.
Results. Clinically oriented analysis showed that on the 30th day of the study, 21 (75%) of the 28 patients in the MG who had moderate menopausal symptoms at the beginning of the study achieved MRS values corresponding to mild or minimal symptoms. In the CG, among 13 patients who had moderate menopausal symptoms at the beginning of the study, similar results were observed in only 4 women (31%). When assessing sexual function, it was found that on the 30th day of the study, 65% women in the MG and 32% patients in the CG had no signs of sexual dysfunction.
Conclusions. The results obtained indicate the potential effectiveness of comprehensive nutritional support as a non-pharmacological approach in women with mild to moderate climacteric symptoms in the MT.

metadata.title: Ricci Flow and the Omega-Frame: A Lens-Relative Reading of Perelman's Approach to the Poincaré Conjecture
metadata.description: This short note does not contain any new geometric results. Its aim is purely explanatory: to read the core dynamical mechanisms in Perelman’s Ricci flow approach to the Poincaré conjecture through the quotient-typed Scan-Reconfigure metatheory developed in the main Omega-Frame paper. Using the checklist headings (Chk1–Chk8) from the main Omega-Frame paper only as loose navigation labels for interface roles (not as verified hypotheses and not as an item-by-item checklist instantiation), we schematically view Ricci flow with surgery as a lens-relative Scan–Reconfigure system equipped with a time skeleton, a monotone potential, a numerical headroom gauge, and an envelope that describes the coarse-grained range of admissible geometric futures. We then suggest that this irreversibility echoes the No Global Two-Sided Rollback pattern studied in the main paper. The note is addressed to readers already familiar with standard expositions of Perelman’s work who wish to see how its structure fits into the unified Omega-Frame/Scan–Reconfigure interface.

metadata.title: The CKM Matrix from the Kähler Class of Fl₁,₂(ℂ³) × ℂP¹
metadata.description:
We derive all four Wolfenstein parameters of the CKM matrix from the Kähler geometry of M = Fl₁,₂(ℂ³) × ℂP¹ within the gradient-flow recording framework. The parameters are determined by four geometric invariants: the Kähler class ratio c₁/c₂ = 4, the Euler characteristic χ(Fl₁,₂(ℂ³)) = 6, the CP asymmetry δ_CP = 3/5, and the fine-structure constant α.

The resulting expressions are:

λ = 0.226 (Cabibbo angle),
A = 5/6,
J = (3/5) α² = 3.20 × 10⁻⁵,
δ = 5 arctan(1/4) = 70.2°.

From these inputs, the full 3 × 3 CKM matrix is reconstructed. All nine matrix elements agree with experimental values to within 5%, with the best-determined entries at sub-percent accuracy.

The structure admits a unified geometric interpretation: scalar transport on the Bruhat decomposition yields the Wolfenstein parameter A, antisymmetric two-root interference yields the Jarlskog invariant J, and Kähler misalignment determines the CP phase δ. The Cabibbo angle follows from the Gatto–Sartori–Tonin relation applied to the framework’s mass spectrum.

The results demonstrate that quark mixing parameters—treated as free inputs in the Standard Model—emerge from fixed geometric data on M, providing a zero-parameter account of flavor mixing consistent with observation.
